2603.19527.title: Depictions of Depression in Generative AI Video Models: A Preliminary Study of OpenAI's Sora 2
2603.19527.paper_summary: Generative video models are increasingly capable of producing complex depictions of mental health experiences, yet little is known about how these systems represent conditions like depression. This study characterizes how OpenAI's Sora 2 generative video model depicts depression and examines whether depictions differ between the consumer App and developer API access points. We generated 100 videos using the single-word prompt "Depression" across two access points: the consumer App (n=50) and developer API (n=50). Two trained coders independently coded narrative structure, visual environments, objects, figure demographics, and figure states. Computational features across visual aesthetics, audio, semantic content, and temporal dynamics were extracted and compared between modalities. App-generated videos exhibited a pronounced recovery bias: 78% (39/50) featured narrative arcs progressing from depressive states toward resolution, compared with 14% (7/50) of API outputs. App videos brightened over time (slope = 2.90 brightness units/second vs. -0.18 for API; d = 1.59, q < .001) and contained three times more motion (d = 2.07, q < .001). Across both modalities, videos converged on a narrow visual vocabulary and featured recurring objects including hoodies (n=194), windows (n=148), and rain (n=83). Figures were predominantly young adults (88% aged 20-30) and nearly always alone (98%). Gender varied by access point: App outputs skewed male (68%), API outputs skewed female (59%). Sora 2 does not invent new visual grammars for depression but compresses and recombines cultural iconographies, while platform-level constraints substantially shape which narratives reach users. Clinicians should be aware that AI-generated mental health video content reflects training data and platform design rather than clinical knowledge, and that patients may encounter such content during vulnerable periods.
2603.19527.paper_summary_zh: 生成視頻模型越來越能夠生產複雜的心理健康經歷描繪，但對於這些系統如何表現抑鬱症等情況知之甚少。這項研究描述了OpenAI的Sora 2生成視頻模型如何描繪抑鬱症，並檢查了消費者應用程序和開發者API接入點之間的描繪是否存在差異。我們使用單詞提示“抑鬱症”在兩個接入點生成了100個視頻：消費者應用程序（n=50）和開發者API（n=50）。兩位受過訓練的編碼員獨立編碼了敘事結構、視覺環境、物體、人物人口統計和人物狀態。從視覺美學、音頻、語義內容和時間動態中提取並比較了計算特徵。應用程序生成的視頻顯示出明顯的恢復偏見：78%（39/50）展示了從抑鬱狀態朝向解決的敘事弧，相較於API輸出的14%（7/50）。應用程序視頻隨著時間變亮（斜率=2.90亮度單位/秒，API為-0.18；d=1.59，q < .001），並且包含了三倍的運動（d=2.07，q < .001）。在兩種模式下，視頻收斂於狹窄的視覺詞彙，並且出現了重複的物體，包括連帽衫（n=194）、窗戶（n=148）和雨（n=83）。人物主要是年輕成年人（88%年齡在20-30歲之間），幾乎總是獨自一人（98%）。性別因接入點而異：應用程序輸出偏向男性（68%），API輸出偏向女性（59%）。Sora 2並不為抑鬱症創造新的視覺語法，而是壓縮並重新組合文化圖標，同時平台級別的限制在很大程度上塑造了哪些敘事能夠到達用戶。臨床醫生應該意識到，AI生成的心理健康視頻內容反映了訓練數據和平台設計，而非臨床知識，並且患者可能在脆弱時期遇到這類內容。
2603.19527.author: Matthew Flathers et.al.
2603.19527.authors: Matthew Flathers, Griffin Smith, Julian Herpertz, Zhitong Zhou, John Torous
